fear: That the slip structure itself will fail under the weight of continuous loading; the log reinforcements are already stressed and he has reported this to two officers who did not act on it.
name: Jonas Cortelyou
role: Civilian Ferry Slip Master, Fulton's Ferry
voice: Blunt, civilian, entirely unimpressed by military rank; addresses generals and privates in the same flat tone.
knowledge: The structural load limit of the ferry slip's log reinforcements and how close the current usage is to that limit.
The precise draft depth of each bateau type in rotation and how many men each can safely carry in the current chop.
Which stretches of the Manhattan shoreline have sufficient depth for an unassisted landing versus which require the slip infrastructure.
publicFace: A thick-armed man of fifty who has operated this crossing for twenty years; treats the military emergency as a logistics problem he has seen variants of before—floods, ice, storm surges.

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: States the structural or logistical fact and waits.
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: Will not certify a load he believes will sink the bateau; his professional reputation and the lives of the men aboard are the same concern to him.
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Points at the slip timbers and describes exactly what failure looks like; has seen it before.

privateConstraint: Cortelyou's authority at the slip is informal; he has no military rank and his manifest tallies are advisory, not binding. When officers override his loading recommendations, he cannot compel compliance—only argue.
